capability_gaps.current_state: No cross-session memory — AI coaching context resets on every session, making longitudinal personalization architecturally impossible
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Lightweight vector or structured memory layer persists user performance history, preferences, and coaching context across sessions within $200/month AI budget
risks: Memory retrieval latency degrades active session UX
Token overhead from context injection blows $200/month AI budget
Cold-start gap persists until minimum viable history is accumulated
Supabase storage schema may require significant migration

capability_gaps.current_state: 74–77% Day-1 abandonment indicates onboarding fails to deliver perceived value before disengagement threshold; no instrumentation to identify exact drop-off point
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Onboarding flow delivers a differentiated, personalized first workout within minutes using minimal input signals; first-session completion exceeds 75%
risks: Over-engineering onboarding length increases friction rather than reducing it
Cold-start personalization feels generic without sufficient early signals
Motivational misalignment if fitness identity framing is wrong at intake
No A/B testing infrastructure to validate onboarding variants solo

capability_gaps.current_state: No commitment devices, loss aversion triggers, variable reward schedules, or habit loop reinforcement are implemented; retention is purely dependent on product utility
capability_gaps.ideal_state: At minimum one commitment device and one variable reward mechanic are live, measurably influencing Day-3 and Day-7 return visits
risks: Poorly calibrated loss aversion mechanics increase anxiety and churn
Gamification layers feel patronizing to high-intent fitness users
Solo implementation risk — behavioral system requires coordinated UX, copy, and timing logic
Without instrumentation, retention mechanic efficacy cannot be measured

capability_gaps.current_state: AI personalization requires longitudinal data that new users haven't yet generated; first-session recommendations are effectively generic
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Cold-start logic uses intake signals (fitness level, goals, equipment, schedule, psychological profile) plus population-level priors to generate a meaningfully differentiated Day-1 workout
risks: Intake form length required for useful priors increases onboarding friction
Population-level priors may not exist without historical user data at current stage
Personalization that feels generic on Day 1 undermines core value proposition
Model inference cost for cold-start logic must be tightly budgeted

capability_gaps.current_state: No automated or data-driven feature prioritization; founder relies on intuition and manual observation with no funnel visibility or behavioral analytics
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Lightweight event tracking surfaces session completion rates, drop-off points, and feature engagement signals that automatically inform sprint priorities
risks: Instrumentation overhead diverts solo engineering time from retention-critical features
Without baseline data, prioritization signals take weeks to accumulate
Over-instrumentation creates data noise rather than signal
Privacy compliance requirements add implementation complexity

capability_gaps.current_state: $200/month AI budget with no documented cost-per-inference tracking, no tiered model selection, and no prompt caching strategy — budget risk increases with user growth
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Cost-per-active-user stays under $0.50/month via prompt caching, haiku-tier routing for lightweight tasks, batched inference, and real-time budget monitoring
risks: Uncapped LLM usage causes budget breach at modest user scale
Aggressive cost cutting degrades coaching quality and perceived AI value
Tiered model routing logic adds engineering complexity for solo founder
No monitoring means budget overruns are discovered after the fact

capability_gaps.area: Exercise Science Grounding for AI Coaching
capability_gaps.current_state: AI coaching outputs are driven by LLM inference without validated exercise science constraints — progressive overload, recovery windows, and contraindications may be inconsistent
capability_gaps.ideal_state: Workout generation is constrained by evidence-based programming rules (progressive overload logic, minimum recovery intervals, movement pattern balance) that the AI cannot override
risks: Injury risk from AI-generated programs lacking biomechanical guardrails creates liability
Overly conservative science constraints reduce perceived AI responsiveness
Exercise catalog gaps limit workout variety for users with equipment constraints
Science layer implementation competes for solo founder engineering time

routing_notes: nova + atlas + algorithm-lab form the critical path triad — memory architecture must ship before behavioral or personalization work can compound; sequence these first
app-experience-studio is the primary onboarding execution agent — route Day-1 UX redesign here before freya or echo add behavioral layers on top
freya owns behavioral economics strategy; quest handles gamification mechanics — keep them coordinated but do not merge; freya sets the retention architecture, quest implements engagement loops
algorithm-lab owns cold-start logic and cost routing — this agent bridges engineering and science domains and should be treated as the AI system design lead
pulse + compass handle instrumentation and prioritization — activate these in parallel with onboarding redesign so baseline data collection begins immediately
workout-program-studio + coaching-architecture-studio handle science grounding as a P2 — do not block P0 shipping on science constraints, but integrate guardrails within two sprints
ledger is Haiku-tier and cost-efficient — route AI budget governance and unit economics questions here, not to nova or atlas

Do not activate haven, herald, beacon, vault, bridge, or shield at this stage — community, PR, ASO, fundraising, partnerships, and compliance are post-retention-fix priorities; activating them now fragments solo founder attention
flow (sports psychology) can be deferred to Cycle 2 — freya covers the behavioral economics core; flow adds motivational depth once retention baseline is established
echo adds neuroscience-informed design value but overlaps significantly with app-experience-studio and freya at this stage — activate only if onboarding redesign stalls on emotional resonance gaps
